You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 542da50f).

## Issue to Resolve

Ignore block comment inside single line block
Give an singe line block containing a block comment than do not wrap this comment to a multiline block. So code below should be kept unchanged:
```
val foo = { /* no-op */ }
```

  8â†’class SpacingAroundCurlyRuleTest {

 11â†’    @Test

 18â†’            """

 22â†’            .hasLintViolations(

 25â†’            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 26â†’    }

 45â†’    }

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

  14â†’internal class WrappingRuleTest {
  15â†’    private val wrappingRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { WrappingRule() }
  16â†’
  17â†’    @Test
  18â†’    fun `Given a multiline string containing a string-template as parameter value but then wrap the value to a start and end on separate lines`() {
  19â†’        // Interpret "$." in code samples below as "$". It is used here as otherwise the indentation in the code sample
  20â†’        // is disapproved when running ktlint on the unit tests during the build process (not that the indent rule can
  21â†’        // not be disabled for a block).
  22â†’        val code =
  23â†’            """
  24â†’            fun foo() {
  25â†’                println("$.{
  26â†’                true
  27â†’                }")
  28â†’            }
  29â†’            """.trimIndent()
  30â†’                .replacePlaceholderWithStringTemplate()
  31â†’        val formattedCode =
  32â†’            """
  33â†’            fun foo() {
  34â†’                println(
  35â†’                    "$.{
  36â†’                        true
  37â†’                    }"
  38â†’                )
  39â†’            }
  40â†’            """.trimIndent()
  41â†’                .replacePlaceholderWithStringTemplate()
  42â†’        wrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
  43â†’            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
  44â†’            .hasLintViolations(
  45â†’                LintViolation(2, 13, "Missing newline after \"(\""),
  46â†’                LintViolation(4, 6, "Missing newline before \")\""),
  47â†’            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
  48â†’    }
  49â†’
  50â†’    @Test
  51â†’    fun `Given a multiline raw string literal then wrap and indent conditionally`() {
  52â†’        val code =
  53â†’            """
  54â†’            fun foo() {
  55â†’                println($MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  56â†’                $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE)
  57â†’                println($MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  58â†’                $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent())
  59â†’                println($MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  60â†’                $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimMargin())
  61â†’            }
  62â†’            """.trimIndent()
  63â†’        val formattedCode =
  64â†’            """
  65â†’            fun foo() {
  66â†’                println(
  67â†’                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  68â†’                $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  69â†’                )
  70â†’                println(
  71â†’                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  72â†’                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
  73â†’                )
  74â†’                println(
  75â†’                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  76â†’                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimMargin()
  77â†’                )
  78â†’            }
  79â†’            """.trimIndent()
  80â†’        wrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
  81â†’            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { IndentationRule() }
  82â†’            .hasLintViolations(
  83â†’                LintViolation(2, 13, "Missing newline after \"(\""),
  84â†’                LintViolation(3, 7, "Missing newline before \")\""),
  85â†’                LintViolation(4, 13, "Missing newline after \"(\""),
  86â†’                LintViolation(5, 20, "Missing newline before \")\""),
  87â†’                LintViolation(6, 13, "Missing newline after \"(\""),
  88â†’                LintViolation(7, 20, "Missing newline before \")\""),
  89â†’            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
  90â†’    }
  91â†’
  92â†’    @Test
  93â†’    fun `Given some multiline raw string literal contain multiline string templates`() {
  94â†’        val code =
  95â†’            """
  96â†’            fun foo1() {

  98â†’            true
  99â†’                }
 100â†’                text
 101â†’            _$.{

 108â†’            """

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.hasNewLineInClosedRange
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 14â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 17â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 18â†’
 19â†’/**
 20â†’ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21â†’ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22â†’ */
 23â†’public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24â†’    StandardRule(
 25â†’        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27â†’            setOf(
 28â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30â†’            ),
 31â†’    ) {
 32â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33â†’        node: ASTNode,
 34â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36â†’    ) {
 37â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39â†’                node
 40â†’                    .prevLeaf()
 41â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43â†’                node
 44â†’                    .nextLeaf()
 45â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46â†’
 47â†’            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48â†’                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49â†’            ) {
 50â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52â†’                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53â†’                    //    some comment
 54â†’                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55â†’                    emit(
 56â†’                        node.startOffset,
 57â†’                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58â†’                            "disallowed",
 59â†’                        false,
 60â†’                    )
 61â†’                } else {
 62â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63â†’                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64â†’                    emit(
 65â†’                        node.startOffset,
 66â†’                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67â†’                        false,
 68â†’                    )
 69â†’                }
 70â†’                return
 71â†’            }
 72â†’
 73â†’            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74â†’                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75â†’
 76â†’            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77â†’                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78â†’        }
 79â†’    }
 80â†’
 81â†’    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85â†’    ) {
 86â†’        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87â†’        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88â†’            emit(
 89â†’                startOffset,
 90â†’                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91â†’                true,
 92â†’            )
 93â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 94â†’                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95â†’            }
 96â†’        } else {
 97â†’            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98â†’            emit(
 99â†’                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100â†’                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101â†’                false,
102â†’            )
103â†’        }
104â†’    }
105â†’
106â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110â†’        rawRemove()
111â†’    }
112â†’
113â†’    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117â†’    ) {
118â†’        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
120â†’            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121â†’        }
122â†’    }
123â†’
124â†’    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125â†’
126â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127â†’}
128â†’
129â†’public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId
130â†’

 26â†’ */
 27â†’public class CommentWrappingRule :
 28â†’    StandardRule(
 29â†’        id = "comment-wrapping",
 30â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 31â†’            setOf(
 32â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 33â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 34â†’            ),
 35â†’    ) {
 36â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 37â†’        node: ASTNode,
 38â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40â†’    ) {
 41â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 42â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 43â†’                node
 44â†’                    .prevLeaf()
 45â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 47â†’                node
 48â†’                    .nextLeaf()
 49â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 50â†’
 51â†’            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 52â†’                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 53â†’            ) {
 54â†’                if (node.prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == LBRACE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == RBRACE) {
 55â†’                    return
 56â†’                }
 57â†’
 58â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 59â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 60â†’                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 61â†’                    //    some comment
 62â†’                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 63â†’                    emit(
 64â†’                        node.startOffset,
 65â†’                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 66â†’                            "disallowed",
 67â†’                        false,
 68â†’                    )
 69â†’                } else {
 70â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 71â†’                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 72â†’                    emit(
 73â†’                        node.startOffset,
 74â†’                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 75â†’                        false,
 76â†’                    )
 77â†’                }
 78â†’                return
 79â†’            }
 80â†’
 81â†’            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 82â†’                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 83â†’
 84â†’            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 85â†’                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 86â†’        }
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 90â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 91â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 92â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 95â†’        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 96â†’            emit(
 97â†’                startOffset,
 98â†’                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 99â†’                true,
100â†’            )
101â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
102â†’                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
103â†’            }
104â†’        } else {
105â†’            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
106â†’            emit(
107â†’                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
108â†’                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
109â†’                false,
110â†’            )
111â†’        }
112â†’    }
113â†’
114â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
115â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
116â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
117â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
118â†’        rawRemove()
119â†’    }
120â†’
121â†’    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
122â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
123â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
124â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
125â†’    ) {
126â†’        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
127â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
128â†’            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
129â†’        }
130â†’    }
131â†’
132â†’    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
133â†’
134â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
135â†’}
136â†’
137â†’public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId
138â†’

conversation_history.content: Fix applied and verified

content.text:
The issue "Ignore block comment inside single line block" was identified as being caused by the `CommentWrappingRule`. This rule was flagging block comments that appeared between other elements on the same line, even when they were inside a single-line block like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`.

I fixed this by modifying `CommentWrappingRule.kt` to explicitly ignore this check if the block comment is immediately preceded by an opening brace (`{`) and immediately followed by a closing brace (`}`), ignoring whitespace and other comments. This preserves the existing behavior for other cases (like `val x = 1 /* comment */ + 2`) while allowing the desired behavior for empty or simple blocks with comments.

I verified the fix by creating a new test file `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingIssueTest.kt` with test cases that reproduce the reported issue. I also ran the existing `CommentWrappingRuleTest` to ensure no regressions were introduced.

**Summary of changes:**
1.  **Modified `CommentWrappingRule.kt`**: Added logic to skip the "block comment in between elements" check if the surrounding code elements are `LBRACE` and `RBRACE`.
2.  **Added `CommentWrappingIssueTest.kt`**: Added a regression test for the reported issue.

I did not modify any existing test files.

Ref: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRule.kt`
Ref: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingIssueTest.kt`

 81â†’    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85â†’    ) {

 87â†’        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {

 89â†’                startOffset,

 91â†’                true,
 92â†’            )
 93â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 94â†’                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95â†’            }
 96â†’        } else {
 97â†’            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98â†’            emit(
 99â†’                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100â†’                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101â†’                false,
102â†’            )
103â†’        }
104â†’    }

106â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110â†’        rawRemove()
111â†’    }

114â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,

116â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117â†’    ) {
118â†’        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)

120â†’            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121â†’        }
122â†’    }

124â†’    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.hasNewLineInClosedRange
 12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent

 14â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf

 16â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 17â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 18â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 20â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 21â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 22â†’
 23â†’/**
 24â†’ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 25â†’ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 26â†’ */
 27â†’public class CommentWrappingRule :
 28â†’    StandardRule(
 29â†’        id = "comment-wrapping",
 30â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 31â†’            setOf(
 32â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 33â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 34â†’            ),
 35â†’    ) {
 36â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 37â†’        node: ASTNode,
 38â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40â†’    ) {
 41â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 42â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 43â†’                node
 44â†’                    .prevLeaf()
 45â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 47â†’                node
 48â†’                    .nextLeaf()
 49â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 50â†’
 51â†’            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 52â†’                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 53â†’            ) {
 54â†’                if (node.prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == LBRACE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == RBRACE) {
 55â†’                    return
 56â†’                }
 57â†’
 58â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 59â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 60â†’                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 61â†’                    //    some comment
 62â†’                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 63â†’                    emit(
 64â†’                        node.startOffset,
 65â†’                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 66â†’                            "disallowed",
 67â†’                        false,
 68â†’                    )
 69â†’                } else {
 70â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 71â†’                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 72â†’                    emit(
 73â†’                        node.startOffset,
 74â†’                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 75â†’                        false,
 76â†’                    )
 77â†’                }
 78â†’                return
 79â†’            }
 80â†’
 81â†’            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 82â†’                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 83â†’
 84â†’            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 85â†’                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 86â†’        }
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 90â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 91â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 92â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 95â†’        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 96â†’            emit(
 97â†’                startOffset,
 98â†’                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 99â†’                true,
100â†’            )
101â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
102â†’                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
103â†’            }
104â†’        } else {
105â†’            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
106â†’            emit(
107â†’                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
108â†’                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
109â†’                false,
110â†’            )
111â†’        }
112â†’    }
113â†’
114â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
115â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
116â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
117â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
118â†’        rawRemove()
119â†’    }
120â†’
121â†’    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
122â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
